name: Hubble tension

observable.description: Present-day expansion rate of the universe. Late-universe distance-ladder measurements (Cepheids + Type Ia supernovae) give a value persistently higher than the value inferred by fitting LambdaCDM to early-universe CMB data.

characteristic_scale.note: Hubble radius ~ c/H_0 ~ 4.4 Gpc. Energy scale is the corresponding Hubble parameter expressed as eV. The tension itself spans the gap between local (Mpc) distance ladder and last-scattering surface (Gpc) physics.

method_variants.label: ACT DR4 + WMAP
method_variants.note: Independent CMB pipeline, consistent with Planck.

method_variants.label: BAO + BBN (no CMB)
method_variants.note: Sound-horizon-anchored inverse distance ladder gives essentially the same low value without CMB.

references: Planck 2018 Results VI: Cosmological Parameters, A&A 641, A6 (2020), arXiv:1807.06209

measurements.reference: Riess et al., ApJL 934, L7 (2022), arXiv:2112.04510

measurements.note: Tip-of-the-Red-Giant-Branch calibration sits between SH0ES and Planck. The choice of distance-ladder rung is itself contested.
JWST imaging confirms HST Cepheid photometry; rules out crowding bias as a major systematic for SH0ES.
Independent of distance ladder. With more conservative lens-mass priors (TDCOSMO 2020), uncertainty grows.

tension_history.note: First time the discrepancy was widely flagged.
JWST data weakened the case that the SH0ES result is a Cepheid systematic. Tension persists.

summary: The locally-measured expansion rate (Cepheid-anchored distance ladder, anchored further by JWST as of 2024) is ~73 km/s/Mpc, while LambdaCDM applied to Planck CMB data and to BAO+BBN inverse distance ladders gives ~67.4. The ~5 sigma discrepancy has survived multiple independent cross-checks. Either there is a systematic in one of the two pipelines that has eluded a decade of scrutiny, or LambdaCDM is incomplete. Proposed solutions (early dark energy, modified recombination, varying fundamental constants) so far each create new tensions elsewhere.
systematics_notes: TRGB-based calibrations sit between the two values, and the Cepheid-vs-TRGB debate is itself active. Time-delay strong lensing gives high values but with large lens-modeling uncertainties. Megamaser distances (e.g., Pesce et al.) also support a high local value. No single early-universe extension to LambdaCDM cleanly resolves H0 without violating BAO, S8, or CMB damping-tail constraints.

references.citation: Riess et al., 'A Comprehensive Measurement of the Local Value of the Hubble Constant with 1 km/s/Mpc Uncertainty from the Hubble Space Telescope and the SH0ES Team', ApJL 934, L7 (2022)
Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', A&A 641, A6 (2020)
Riess et al., 'JWST Observations Reject Unrecognized Crowding of Cepheid Photometry as an Explanation for the Hubble Tension at 8 sigma Confidence', ApJL 962, L17 (2024)
Freedman et al., 'Status Report on the Chicago-Carnegie Hubble Program (CCHP)' (2024)
Di Valentino et al., 'In the Realm of the Hubble Tension - A Review of Solutions', Class. Quantum Grav. 38, 153001 (2021)
